entries.definition: To increase in size, volume, or quantity, often by internal pressure; to become larger or rounder; to grow in intensity or importance.
entries.exampleSentence: Her ankle began to swell after she twisted it.

entries.definition: A gradual increase in the size or volume of the sea or waves; a long rolling wave or series of waves; a gradual increase or rise.
entries.exampleSentence: A swell rolled in from the open ocean, making the boat rock gently.
They had a swell time at the picnic.
